root_causes.cause: Router R2 is not advertising its LAN (172.16.0.0/24) into OSPF, so R1 (and the rest of the network) have no route to 172.16.0.11.
evidence: R2 interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0 configured with address 172.16.0.1/24 (get_running_config for R2).
R2 OSPF process lists network statements for 10.0.0.0 and 10.0.0.4 only (get_running_config/get_ospf for R2) — no 172.16.0.0/24 advertised.
OSPF adjacencies between routers are up (neighbors in ospf-nbr-full), indicating control-plane connectivity is OK; failure is consistent with missing route advertisement rather than adjacency loss.

plan.problem: Router R2 is not advertising 172.16.0.0/24 into OSPF, so other routers have no route to 172.16.0.11. Assumptions/missing_info: OSPF process ID is 1, area is 0, and device hostname 'R2' maps to controller device 'router2'.
plan.fix_summary: Add the 172.16.0.0/24 advertisement to R2's OSPF configuration so the network is propagated to peers.

R2 OSPF process lists network statements for 10.0.0.0 and 10.0.0.4 only (get_running_config/get_ospf for R2) — no 172.16.0.0/24 advertised.
OSPF adjacencies between routers are up (neighbors in ospf-nbr-full), indicating control-plane connectivity is OK; failure is consistent with missing route advertisement rather than adjacency loss.

plan.problem: Router R2 is not advertising 172.16.0.0/24 into OSPF, so other routers have no route to 172.16.0.11. Assumptions/missing_info: OSPF process ID is 1, area is 0, and device hostname 'R2' maps to controller device 'router2'.
plan.fix_summary: Add the 172.16.0.0/24 advertisement to R2's OSPF configuration so the network is propagated to peers.

messages.content:
What was observed
- Source 192.168.0.11 could not reach 172.16.0.11 (pings failing; destination unreachable).

Diagnosis (root cause)
- Router R2 was not advertising its LAN 172.16.0.0/24 into OSPF, so other routers had no route to 172.16.0.11.

Changes made
- On R2 added OSPF advertisement for 172.16.0.0/24 (router ospf 1 → network 172.16.0.0 0.0.0.255 area 0).

Verification
- Passed: ping from R1 to 172.16.0.11 succeeded (5/5, 100%); OSPF adjacencies on all routers are full.
- Remaining: none identified. Connectivity to 172.16.0.11 restored.
